item.title: Oil prices drop on hopes of US pullback from Iran war
item.description: Oil prices fell more than $1 early on Thursday as markets awaited an address to the nation ​by President Donald Trump that could signal a U.S. ‌pullback from the war in Iran.

item.title: Trump says US has plenty of jet fuel for Europe, market disagrees
item.description: President Donald Trump this week told countries struggling to get jet ​fuel due to Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz to buy from the U.S., but analysts say there is ‌a major problem with his advice: the U.S. can't cover the global shortfall.

item.title: Stocks, currencies on tenterhooks ahead of Trump address on Iran war
item.description: Stocks inched higher, the dollar softened and oil slipped on Thursday as investors held their collective breath ahead of a speech from U.S. President Donald Trump that could outline the end of ​the war in the Middle East and boost risk appetite.

item.title: Trump administration readies new tariffs on select drugmakers, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: The Trump administration is set to ​announce tariffs as soon as Thursday ‌on drugmakers that have not struck deals guaranteeing low prices in the U.S., Bloomberg News ​reported on Wednesday, citing people ​familiar with the plan.

item.title: Hedge funds face worst monthly drawdown in over four years, Goldman Sachs tells clients
item.description: Global hedge funds last month faced their worst monthly drawdowns since January ​2022, Goldman Sachs said in a client note on Wednesday, as market volatility triggered by the Iran war battered stocks and weighed on ‌the performance of the world's biggest money managers.

item.title: Auto industry group calls for scrapping US gas tax, adopting vehicle fee
item.description: The head of a group representing nearly all major automakers on Wednesday called for scrapping ​the U.S. government's 18.4-cent per gallon tax on gasoline and replacing it ‌with a vehicle fee to pay for road repairs.

item.title: Amazon in talks to buy $9 billion satellite group Globalstar, FT reports
item.description: Amazon is ​in talks to ‌acquire satellite telecommunications group Globalstar , the Financial Times ​reported on Wednesday, ​citing people familiar with ⁠the matter.

item.title: Exclusive: Intel looks to put millions more into SambaNova startup chaired by CEO Tan
item.description: Chipmaker Intel has planned to invest another $15 million in SambaNova, a chip startup chaired by Intel CEO Lip-Bu ​Tan, a Reuters review of corporate records showed.

item.title: Australia to offer businesses $693 million in cheap loans to ease fuel cost pressure
item.description: Australia will provide up to A$1 billion ($693 million) in interest-free loans to critical businesses, including transport operators ​and fertiliser producers, Prime Minister Anthony Albanese will say ‌on Thursday, as they grapple with surging fuel costs.

item.title: US Treasury to meet with insurance regulators to discuss private credit markets
item.description: The U.S. Treasury Department said ​on Wednesday that it will convene ‌meetings starting in April with domestic and international insurance regulators focused on recent developments ​in private credit markets.

item.title: KKR caps redemptions at one of its private credit funds
item.description: KKR's non-traded business development company, KKR FS Income Trust, limited redemptions after requests ​for withdrawals surged, according to a letter to ‌shareholders.

item.title: Australia's regulator flags governance, risk failures at ASX in final report
item.description: Australia's corporate regulator on Thursday flagged governance weaknesses and risk management failures ​at the country's market operator ASX in its final report ‌following an independent inquiry.

item.title: Automakers plan billions in US investments but seek clear trade rules
item.description: Global automakers plan billions of dollars in new U.S. investments to boost production and avoid President Donald Trump's tariffs, but they are awaiting clarity on the ​status of a North American free trade agreement and future vehicle duties.

item.title: TRADING DAY April fool of hope
item.description: Stocks rose on Wednesday and oil and the dollar fell on continued optimism that de-escalation in the Middle East is imminent. U.S. President Donald Trump said the U.S. will leave Iran pretty quickly, and attention now turns to his televised address later on Wednesday evening.

item.title: Watch: NASA’s Artemis II crew set to begin historic launch mission toward the moon
item.description: NASA is set to launch four astronauts on a 10-day flight around the moon, marking the most ambitious U.S. space mission in decades.

item.title: US trade panel to probe Roku, Hisense streaming and display imports for patent violations
item.description: The U.S. International Trade Commission said on ​Wednesday that it has ‌opened a Section 337 patent violation investigation into certain ​Roku and Hisense brand ​display devices, streaming players and ⁠components.

item.title: Estee Lauder, Puig advance talks for a stock‑based deal, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: U.S. cosmetics giant ​Estee Lauder and Spanish firm ‌Puig are advancing talks to combine in a deal that would consist ​mostly of stock, Bloomberg News ​reported on Wednesday, citing people ⁠familiar with the matter.

item.title: WhatsApp says Italian surveillance company tricked around 200 users into downloading spyware
item.description: Messaging service WhatsApp said an Italian surveillance company tricked some 200 ​users into downloading a bogus version of ‌its popular app that was rigged to spy on its victims.

item.title: Colombia government rift with central bank clouds future rate decisions, analysts say
item.description: Colombia faces mounting uncertainty ‌after the government's decision to withdraw from the central bank's board cast doubt on future decisions following a 100-basis-point interest rate hike, analysts warned on Wednesday.

item.title: SpaceX’s orbital data centers could face same hurdles as Microsoft’s abandoned undersea project
item.description: SpaceX on Wednesday filed for an IPO that Elon Musk says will bankroll an effort to turn the rocket maker into an AI powerhouse, launching up to 1 million data‑center satellites into orbit to bypass power and water limits on Earth.

item.title: Related Digital nears $16 billion financing for Oracle data center, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: Data center developer Related Digital is closing in on $16 billion of financing for a giant ​Oracle data center following months of negotiations with ‌investors, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday.

item.title: Bank of Canada says it will rely on judgment more than usual for rate decisions
item.description: By Promit Mukherjee and David Ljunggren

item.title: SpaceX IPO buzz lifts aerospace shares on spillover bets
item.description: Shares of aerospace companies jumped on Wednesday as investors bet that Elon Musk-owned SpaceX's confidential filing for an initial public offering would be ​a catalyst for the sector's next growth phase.

item.title: California, states seek $10.3 million for helping block Kroger-Albertsons merger
item.description: California and ​a group of states are seeking $10.3 million in fees and costs ‌for helping block the Kroger-Albertsons merger, a bid that would refill the state's coffers as it emerges as a major player in U.S. antitrust enforcement.

item.title: Exclusive: SpaceX will host analyst day on April 21, source says
item.description: Rocket company SpaceX ‌will host an analyst day ​on April ​21, and will offer ⁠analysts a ​visit to its "Macrohard" ​xAI datacenter in Memphis, Tennessee two days ​later, a ​source familiar with the ‌matter ⁠said on Wednesday.

item.title: US financial advisors brace for growing array of risks in second quarter
item.description: Investment advisors say a buildup of problems is weighing on clients, who are entering the ​second quarter of the year struggling with trying to predict the outcome of war, the direction of energy prices and the repercussions of ‌problems stemming from private credit.

item.title: Unilever CEO Fernandez returns to his roots with health and beauty makeover
item.description: As a senior Unilever executive in Brazil some 15 years ago, Fernando Fernandez made a bold gamble on hair care ​and beauty, rapidly expanding the then newly acquired TRESemmé brand into a major money-spinner in the giant South American market.

item.title: Italian bill proposes curbs on social media addiction
item.description: Italian senators on Wednesday presented a draft law to tackle social media addiction that seeks to increase the responsibility ​of online platforms for the way content is distributed to ‌users.

item.title: Lilly's weight-loss pill wins US approval
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration on Wednesday ​approved Eli Lilly's weight-loss pill, making it the second to ‌market after Novo Nordisk's oral Wegovy, as the companies fight to capture more customers with the convenience of pills over injectables.

item.title: SpaceX's business and finances: rockets, satellite communications and budding AI
item.description: SpaceX is gearing up for a stock market debut that could value it at more than $1.75 trillion, as the Elon Musk-led company reaches for the stars in what could be the largest IPO in history.
